key_insight: Math reasoning in multilingual LLMs is supported by partially shared parameters across languages, with the strongest overlap appearing in intermediate layers rather than being fully language-invariant or language-specific.
engineering_relevance: For multilingual model design and adaptation, language-aware parameter sharing may preserve math capability better than assuming one universal set of weights or separate per-language modules.
method: Localized and compared math-associated parameters across languages in an LLM.
Identified cross-lingual overlap patterns across model layers.
Compared parameter sets for English versus lower-resource languages.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a mechanistic cross-lingual analysis showing that English yields the largest set of math-relevant parameters, while lower-resource languages produce smaller sets.
watch_item: The results are based on extracted parameter overlap from the abstracted analysis, so practical gains may depend on how well these identified parameters transfer to downstream multilingual math tasks.
paper_title: LLM Parameters for Math Across Languages: Shared or Separate?
